title: Crispy Parmesan Potatoes with Fish Fingers & Tartar Sauce
summary: Golden, cheesy roasties with crispy fish fingers all baked in one tray with a tangy dill tartar sauce to finish. It’s like a nostalgic fish and chip night, only way better.

method.description: Heat the oven to 210°C. Finely grate the parmesan. Halve the potatoes and toss them in a bowl with the olive oil, a generous pinch of salt and some black pepper.

Dip the cut side of each potato into the parmesan, pressing to stick a thin layer of cheese onto each one. Arrange on the tray, cheese-side down, leaving space for the fish fingers. Nestle the fish fingers around the tray and and bake until both are golden and crispy and the potatoes are tender, about 30 mins.
Meanwhile, zest the lemon into a bowl. Chop half of the dill and finely dice the cornichons. Stir through the yoghurt along with the Dijon mustard. Season to taste, you want it punchy.
Cook the peas following pack instructions.
Cut the lemon into 4 wedges. Serve up the crispy potatoes and fish fingers with a big spoonful of tartar sauce, a lemon wedge and the peas on the side. Sprinkle over the remaining dill. Dig in.

chefs.name: Anna Helm Baxter
chefs.summary: Anna Helm Baxter is a cookbook author, food stylist, and food producer at Mob. Having spent most of her life in the US, she loves potlucks, tailgates, and a good Thanksgiving spread. As a working mum of three, she gets the dinner struggle, which is why she’s all about recipes that are joyful, healthy, and totally doable. And if all else fails she always has cookies stashed in the freezer.
